tasks.name: Pilot: Pipeline Validation and L0 Gate
tasks.description: Load Gemma 2 2B + Gemma Scope SAEs at layer 12 widths 16k and 65k. Run sae-spelling on letters A-E (n=100 tokens each, seed 42). Measure empirical L0 for both widths on 500-token sample. Compute alpha_ij for top-100 candidate pairs by decoder cosine similarity on 1k activations; log top-10 alpha_ij pairs and verify whether any correspond to letter-feature parent-child relationships. Write go/no-go gate decision to exp/results/pilots/P0_pipeline.json. Decision gate: absorption rate in [10%, 45%] AND top-10 alpha_ij contains >= 1 letter-feature pair. If L0 difference > 20% between widths, set flag 'l0_confound=true' in output for downstream use.

pilot.pass_criteria: absorption_rate in [0.05, 0.50] for at least 3 of 5 letters AND sae-spelling pipeline executes without errors AND at least 1 alpha_ij pair in top-10 is a known letter-feature parent-child

visualizations.description: Pilot validation summary: absorption rate per letter, L0 for each SAE width

tasks.name: Component 1A: Activation Statistics and Alpha_ij Computation
tasks.description: For each SAE configuration (Gemma 2 2B, layers {6, 12, 20, 25}, widths {16k, 65k}), run 10k OpenWebText tokens through the SAE using SAELens. Record: per-latent mean activation frequency f_i (fraction of tokens where latent > 0); pairwise co-activation rate P(a_i>0, a_j>0) for all pairs where both f_i>0.001 and f_j>0.001 AND decoder cosine similarity > 0.15. Compute sigma_ij = P(a_i>0, a_j>0) / min(f_i, f_j) and alpha_ij = sigma_ij * (f_j / f_i). Save parquet output per SAE config. This is the foundation for all H1 and H4 analyses.

pilot.scope: layer 12 only, width 16k only, 1k tokens instead of 10k
pilot.pass_criteria: alpha_ij parquet file written with >= 500 valid pairs AND no NaN/inf values in alpha_ij column AND at least 10 pairs with alpha_ij > 1.0

visualizations.description: Distribution of alpha_ij values across all candidate pairs

tasks.name: Component 1B: LV Detector Threshold Calibration and Validation (H1)
tasks.description: Load sae-spelling ground-truth absorption labels for Gemma Scope 16k layer 12, all 26 letters (n=100 test tokens per letter, seed 42). Split letters A-M as calibration (13 letters), N-Z as test (13 letters). For calibration letters: load alpha_ij from C1A output; for each absorbed pair, look up alpha_ij value; for each non-absorbed pair (sampled as negatives, ratio 3:1), look up alpha_ij. Fit threshold tau via F1 maximization over tau in {0.5, 0.75, 1.0, 1.25, 1.5}. Apply best tau to test letters; report precision, recall, F1, ROC-AUC. Also compute LV sharpness diagnostic: bin alpha_ij in bins of 0.1 over [0, 3], compute empirical absorption rate per bin, fit both sigmoid and linear models, compare AIC. Save full results to exp/results/full/C1B_lv_validation.json.

pilot.scope: calibration on letters A-E only (5 letters instead of 13); test on letters F-H only (3 letters)
pilot.pass_criteria: F1 > 0.35 on 3-letter pilot test set (below full criterion but confirms pipeline works) AND sigmoid fit converges without error AND AIC values are finite

visualizations.description: Main H1 validation: precision, recall, F1, AUC for LV detector vs cosine-only baseline
LV sharpness test: absorption rate vs alpha_ij bins with sigmoid and linear fits

tasks.name: Component 1C: Cross-Architecture Validation of LV Detector
tasks.description: Apply the calibrated tau from C1B (no re-fitting) to two alternative SAE architectures at Gemma 2 2B layer 12: (1) Gemma Scope JumpReLU SAEs (check SAELens release list for availability), (2) Gemma Scope TopK SAEs if available. For each architecture: run same activation stats collection as C1A (10k tokens, same pre-filter), compute alpha_ij, apply fixed tau, compare predicted absorbed pairs against sae-spelling ground truth labels for that architecture. Report F1 and F1 degradation vs. within-architecture calibration. If JumpReLU or TopK not available via SAELens, use second L0 setting of standard SAEs as substitute and document this.

pilot.scope: JumpReLU SAEs only (skip TopK); use letters A-E only for validation
pilot.pass_criteria: JumpReLU SAE loads successfully via SAELens AND F1 is computable (i.e., alpha_ij scores are non-degenerate for JumpReLU) AND F1 degradation < 30 percentage points (lenient criterion for pilot)

visualizations.description: Cross-architecture F1 comparison: standard vs JumpReLU vs TopK

tasks.name: Component 1D: Width Paradox — DAS(k=1) and DAS(k=3) vs SAE Width (H4)
tasks.description: For Gemma Scope SAEs at widths {16k, 65k, 131k}, layer 12 (same layer as H1 validation for consistency). For each width: (1) run sae-spelling to get DAS(k=1) absorption rates for all 26 letter-feature parents; (2) from C1A alpha_ij outputs, identify top-3 children C1, C2, C3 per parent by alpha_ij value; (3) compute DAS(P, k=3) via multi-label logistic regression: fit logistic regression predicting binary parent activation from {f_C1, f_C2, f_C3} on 10k activation samples; DAS(k=3) = 1 - McFadden_R2_of_3child_model / McFadden_R2_of_baseline. Report DAS(k=1) and DAS(k=3) per letter per width. Compute linear regression slope of DAS(k=3) vs log(width) across {16k, 65k, 131k} for each letter. Report fraction of letters with positive slope for k=3 and non-positive slope for k=1.

pilot.scope: widths {16k, 65k} only (skip 131k until 16k/65k show expected pattern); letters A-E only
pilot.pass_criteria: DAS(k=1) and DAS(k=3) both computable for all 5 pilot letters at both widths AND DAS(k=3) >= DAS(k=1) for at least 3 of 5 letters at 65k (distributed > concentrated at wider SAE)

visualizations.description: Width paradox figure: DAS(k=1) and DAS(k=3) vs SAE width across letters

tasks.name: Component 2A: PMI Extraction from OpenWebText (CPU task)
tasks.description: Extract corpus PMI features for all 26 letter-token pairs from a 1M-token OpenWebText sample. Steps: (1) Load tokenized OpenWebText using HuggingFace datasets (Skylion007/openwebtext or stas/openwebtext-10k); tokenize with Gemma 2 tokenizer; take first 1M tokens. (2) For each of the 26 letters: compute marginal token frequency P(token_starts_with_L) across the corpus; compute joint frequency P(child_token, context_within_5_tokens) using a sliding window; compute PMI = log[ P(child|context) / P(child) ] for each specific child token with its parent letter category. (3) Save to exp/results/full/C2A_pmi_features.json with columns: [letter, token_id, token_str, pmi_score, child_frequency, parent_frequency]. This is a CPU task — no GPU needed.

pilot.scope: Use first 100k tokens instead of 1M; compute PMI for letters A-E only
pilot.pass_criteria: PMI values computed for >= 5 distinct child tokens per pilot letter AND PMI values span range [-3, 5] (not degenerate) AND no NaN/inf in output

visualizations.description: Top PMI child tokens per letter (A-E), showing which tokens are most associated with each letter category

tasks.name: Component 2B: 30-SAE Absorption Survey
tasks.description: Run sae-spelling absorption measurement on 30 Gemma Scope SAE configurations to build the regression dataset for H2. Configurations: widths {16k, 65k, 131k} x layers {6, 12, 20, 25} x L0 settings {low, medium} where available = up to 24 unique configs; augment with additional L0 settings at layer 12 (most data) to reach ~30 total. For each config: run sae-spelling on all 26 letters, n=100 test tokens per letter, seed 42. Record absorption_rate per (SAE config, letter). Save merged dataset to exp/results/full/C2B_absorption_survey.parquet. NOTE: This is the longest single task (~8 GPU-hours in proposal). Consider parallelizing across width groups if multiple GPU slots available; otherwise run sequentially. Estimated time assumes 30 configs x ~15 min each = 450 min total; can be split into 3 subtasks if needed.

pilot.scope: 3 configs only: (layer=12, width=16k, L0=low), (layer=12, width=65k, L0=medium), (layer=20, width=16k, L0=low); letters A-E only
pilot.pass_criteria: absorption_rate measured for all 5 pilot letters x 3 configs (15 data points) AND absorption rates vary across configs (std > 0.02) AND at least 2 configs show absorption > 0.10 for at least 1 letter

visualizations.description: Absorption rate heatmap: 30 SAE configs (rows) x 26 letters (columns)

tasks.notes: LONG TASK (8 hrs). Consider splitting C2B into three subtasks by layer group if a second GPU slot becomes available: C2B_layer6_12, C2B_layer20_25, C2B_extra_l0. Each subtask ~160 min.

tasks.name: Component 2C: PMI Regression Analysis (H2)
tasks.description: Load C2B absorption survey parquet and C2A PMI features. Merge on (letter, SAE config). Fit regression model: absorption_rate_il = beta0 + beta1*log(L0_i) + beta2*log(width_i) + beta3*layer_i + beta4*log(PMI_l) + epsilon_il. Use OLS regression with HC3 heteroskedasticity-robust standard errors. Report: R^2, adjusted R^2, partial R^2 for PMI term (from partial regression), p-value for beta4, coefficient signs for all terms. Visualize partial regression plot of absorption_rate vs log(PMI) with SAE config residualized. Ablation A3/A4: also fit PMI-only model and per-layer regression. Save results to exp/results/full/C2C_regression_results.json.

pilot.scope: Use pilot data from C2B (3 configs x 5 letters = 15 data points) and C2A (5 letters PMI). Fit simplified regression: absorption_rate = beta0 + beta1*log(L0) + beta4*log(PMI). Note: 15 data points is too few for reliable inference; pilot only checks that regression runs without error and PMI coefficient has expected positive sign.
pilot.pass_criteria: Regression executes without error AND beta4 (PMI coefficient) is positive AND regression R^2 is finite

visualizations.description: Partial regression plot: absorption rate residuals vs log(PMI) residuals; each point is one (SAE config, letter) cell

tasks.name: Component 2D: Absorption Taxonomy Type I/II/III (H5)
tasks.description: Operationalize and measure three absorption types on Gemma Scope 16k layer 12. For all 26 letter features: (1) Type I (Full): Chanin metric > 0.5 AND single absorbing latent accounts for > 80% of parent suppression (measured via activation magnitude ratio). (2) Type II (Partial): Parent latent activation at < 50% expected magnitude on expected-parent-token set; 'expected magnitude' is median activation of the latent on the non-absorbed comparison set. (3) Type III (Distributed): DAS(k=3) > 0.60 AND Type I not triggered. Each letter feature is classified as one type (Type I takes priority, then II, then III, then None). Report: count and fraction of 26 features in each type; comprehensive absorption rate = fraction with Type I OR II OR III. Compare to Chanin et al. 15-35% Type I rate. Save to exp/results/full/C2D_taxonomy.json.

pilot.scope: Letters A-E only; Type I from existing C1B output; Type II and III computed on pilot
pilot.pass_criteria: All three type classification rules execute without error AND at least 1 letter classified as each type (or at least 2 different types observed across A-E) AND comprehensive rate for pilot letters exceeds Type I rate for same letters

visualizations.description: Absorption taxonomy: Type I/II/III/None fraction per SAE width (16k, 65k, 131k)

tasks.name: Component 3A: SAEBench Downstream Correlation Analysis (H3 Stage 1, CPU)
tasks.description: Download SAEBench CSV from neuronpedia.org/sae-bench (or via HuggingFace if available). Filter to Gemma 2 2B SAEs. For each available downstream task (RAVEL, SCR, sparse probing F1, unlearning): compute Pearson r and Spearman rho between absorption_score column and task performance. Apply Bonferroni correction for 4 tests (alpha_corrected = 0.0125). Compute partial Pearson r after controlling for: SAE width (log), layer, architecture class (dummy variable). Report full correlation matrix, 95% CIs, corrected p-values, and partial correlations. Flag pre-specified 'meaningful' threshold: |r| > 0.30. Save to exp/results/full/C3A_saebench_corr.json. This is a CPU-only task with zero GPU required.

pilot.scope: Download SAEBench CSV; compute Pearson r for absorption_score vs RAVEL only (single task); check whether CSV loads and absorption_score column exists
pilot.pass_criteria: SAEBench CSV downloads successfully AND absorption_score column present AND RAVEL column present AND Pearson r is finite (correlation computed on >= 10 SAEs)

visualizations.description: Correlation matrix: absorption score vs downstream task performance metrics with significance markers

tasks.name: Component 3B: Matched RAVEL Comparison (H3 Stage 2)
tasks.description: From SAEBench data (C3A output), select top-5 lowest absorption SAEs and top-5 highest absorption SAEs matched on: model = Gemma 2 2B, layer in {12, 20}. Run RAVEL evaluation directly on each matched pair using SAEBench evaluation code (github.com/adamkarvonen/SAEBench). For each of the 10 selected SAEs: run RAVEL task and record performance score. Perform paired t-test (one-sided, H0: low-absorption SAEs do not outperform high-absorption SAEs on RAVEL), alpha = 0.05. Also report Cohen's d as effect size. Save to exp/results/full/C3B_matched_ravel.json.

pilot.scope: Select top-2 lowest and top-2 highest absorption SAEs (4 total instead of 10); run RAVEL on 50 prompts per SAE instead of full eval
pilot.pass_criteria: RAVEL evaluation code runs without error AND produces numeric scores for all 4 pilot SAEs AND scores differ by > 0.01 between any two SAEs (not degenerate constant output)

visualizations.description: RAVEL performance distribution for low-absorption vs high-absorption SAE groups

tasks.name: Component 3C: Safety Probe Pilot (H3 Stage 3)
tasks.description: Build binary harmful intent classification using 3 Gemma 2 2B SAEs at lowest, median, and highest absorption rates (from C3A data). Dataset: 50 harmful prompts from AdvBench subset + 50 benign prompts from OpenWebText. For each of the 3 SAEs: (1) Dense linear probe: run prompts through Gemma 2 2B, extract residual stream at layer 12, train logistic regression classifier. (2) 1-sparse SAE probe: encode residual stream through SAE, constrain to top-1 most active feature (L0=1 equivalent), train logistic regression. For each probe type on each SAE: compute ROC-AUC via 5-fold cross-validation. Report: AUC_dense vs AUC_1sparse per absorption level; does probe gap (AUC_dense - AUC_1sparse) decrease as absorption decreases? Save to exp/results/full/C3C_safety_probe.json.

pilot.scope: 1 SAE only (lowest absorption); 20 harmful + 20 benign prompts; dense probe only (skip 1-sparse for pilot speed); 3-fold cross-validation
pilot.pass_criteria: Logistic regression trains without error AND ROC-AUC > 0.50 (better than random) for dense probe on pilot data AND no NaN in cross-validation scores

visualizations.description: ROC curves: dense probe vs 1-sparse SAE probe at low/median/high absorption SAEs

tasks.name: Component 1 Ablations: Threshold Sensitivity and Cosine Pre-filter (A1, A2)
tasks.description: Run ablation experiments for H1 detector: (A1) Threshold sensitivity: re-evaluate H1 LV detector at tau values {0.5, 0.75, 1.0, 1.25, 1.5} on the 13-letter test set; report F1 for each tau; confirm F1 peak is within {0.75, 1.25}. (A2) Cosine pre-filter threshold: re-run C1A with cosine thresholds {0.10, 0.15, 0.25}; for each threshold, compute coverage (fraction of known absorbed pairs that have a candidate pair in the pre-filtered set) and precision; confirm >= 80% of absorbed pairs are captured at threshold=0.15. Also compare LV detector (alpha_ij > tau) vs cosine-only baseline (cosine > threshold) at matched precision; report F1 delta. Save to exp/results/full/C1_ablations.json.

pilot.scope: A1 only (threshold sweep); use letters N-Z test set from C1B; run 5 tau values
pilot.pass_criteria: F1 values computed for all 5 tau values AND F1 is non-zero for at least 3 tau values AND results written to output file without error

visualizations.description: F1 vs threshold tau for LV detector vs cosine-only baseline

parallel_groups.description: Independent parallel start: pilot validation, PMI extraction, SAEBench download
parallel_groups.notes: C2A and C3A require no GPU and can run in parallel with P0 on CPU

parallel_groups.description: After P0 passes gate: activation stats collection (unblocks H1, H4 tasks)
After C1A: LV detector + 30-SAE survey can run in parallel if 2 GPUs available
parallel_groups.notes: C2B is the longest task (480 min); if only 1 GPU, run C1B first then C2B

parallel_groups.description: After C1B: cross-architecture + width paradox in parallel with C3A downstream
After C2B and C2A: regression analysis (CPU-only, fast)
After C1D and C2B: taxonomy; after C3A: matched RAVEL and safety probe
